project.name: HSM Attestation Reference Implementation
project.description:
Reference implementation for HSM attestation verification with CSR and BankID signing. Implements the financial-entity side of the four-phase supervisory issuance flow described in Gillström (in preparation, CMLJ and CLSR): local verification, gatekeeper.verify, issuance, and gatekeeper.confirm. The companion repository gatekeeper carries the supervisory side.
Legal basis (Union law): DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — substantive obligations on the financial entity: Articles 5(2)(b) (management body's authenticity/integrity duty), 6(10) (financial entity remains fully responsible for the verification of compliance — the central article this code implements support for), 9(2), 9(3)(b)(c)(d), 9(4)(d) (ICT-security control objectives), 16 (vulnerability management — supported by the bundled OWASP Dependency-Check plugin), 28(1)(a) (full responsibility irrespective of outsourcing), 28(6) (retention of ICT third-party records — minimum 5 years for the issuance audit trail represented by IssuanceResponse), 30(2)(c) (contractual provisions on data protection; Article 1 in the companion paper Gillström (in preparation) argues that an HSM clause without verification mechanism does not satisfy this provision). DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — supervisory consequences for the financial entity: Article 33(3) (criteria the Lead Overseer applies to ICT third- party providers — the financial entity's audit record must be capable of supporting the supervisor's assessment); Article 42 (competent authorities follow up on Lead-Overseer recommendations; the financial entity's IssuanceResponse audit record is the evidence the competent authority will inspect); Article 50 (administrative powers — access to documents and on-site inspection extend to the financial entity). BankID — IMPORTANT: the BankID-signature verification layer implemented by BankIdService is NOT a DORA-mandated control. BankID is the Swedish eID that Getswish AB uses for signatory authentication; technical providers integrating with Swish implement BankID-signature verification because that is the operational precondition for certificate issuance, not because DORA prescribes it. DORA mandates HSM-protected key origin (Articles 6(10), 9, 28(1)(a)) — the cryptographic-protection layer in Step 5 of the verification pipeline. It does not specify how the requesting organisation's signatory is authenticated. The bundled BankID verifier therefore handles an integration-side requirement, not a regulatory one. A future change in which eID Swish uses replaces this layer without affecting DORA compliance.
eIDAS Regulation (EU) 910/2014 — provides only the legal-effect framing for the BankID signatures handled by the contractual mandate-verification layer above; explicitly does NOT apply to the technical provider's HSM-attested payment-signing keys (Swish payment signatures are operational signatures over payment transactions, not eIDAS-recognised legal-act signatures). Articles invoked indirectly: Article 25 (legal effects of electronic signatures — frames the legal weight of a BankID-signed mandate when it is presented as evidence in a downstream dispute); Article 26 (advanced electronic signature requirements — BankID typically meets the AES bar); Article 29 (qualified electronic signature requirements — whether a particular BankID signature reaches QES status depends on whether the BankID-issuing CA is on the EU Trusted List under Article 22; this code does not enforce or assume QES status); Annex II (qualified signature creation device — relevant if a deployment uses BankID-on-card or similar QSCD-based BankID issuance; the Primus HSM used elsewhere in the Swish payment infrastructure is QSCD-capable but the qualified- signature mode is NOT activated, see HARDWARE_BASELINE).
The mTLS layer between this financial entity and the technical provider's Swish API is operational TLS under DORA Article 9 and is governed by the partner agreement — distinct both from the BankID layer above and from any eIDAS qualified-signature status. GDPR Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — Articles 5(1)(c)(e), 17, 23, 32. NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — Articles 14, 21, 23.
Legal basis (Swedish national law): Cybersäkerhetslag (2025:1506) — NIS2 implementation; defines reporting and supervision obligations applicable to the financial entity Lag (2010:751) om betaltjänster — payment service provider obligations Lag (2017:630) om åtgärder mot penningtvätt och finansiering av terrorism — AML obligations relevant to the BankID signatory chain Bokföringslag (1999:1078) — 7 kap. retention of business records (parallel to the audit-evidence retention requirements)
